title: Drip vs Sendinblue (2025): Which Is Better?
meta_description: Compare Drip and Sendinblue email marketing tools. See pricing, features, and which is best for your business needs.
h1: Drip vs Sendinblue: Detailed Comparison for Email Marketing
intro: Drip and Sendinblue (now Brevo) are both popular email marketing platforms, but they serve different business needs. Drip specializes in e-commerce automation with deep store integration, while Sendinblue offers a freemium all-in-one CRM suite with SMS and WhatsApp. Choosing between them depends on your budget, business type, and required features.

comparison_table.tool_a: E-commerce tracking, revenue attribution, onsite campaigns
comparison_table.tool_b: Basic email/SMS, no specialized e-commerce tools

choose_a_if: You run an e-commerce business and need advanced tracking and revenue attribution
You want specialized automation features built specifically for online stores
Deep integration with your ecommerce platform is a priority
choose_b_if: You're looking for a free or low-cost email marketing solution to start
You need an all-in-one CRM with multiple communication channels including WhatsApp
You want to add transactional email, SMS, and chat to your existing business tools

faq.a: It depends on your needs. Drip is superior for e-commerce businesses requiring detailed tracking and revenue attribution, while Sendinblue excels as a budget-friendly, all-in-one CRM solution. Sendinblue has higher customer satisfaction ratings and more versatile communication channels.

faq.q: Which is cheaper, Drip or Sendinblue?
faq.a: Sendinblue is significantly cheaper for small businesses, offering a free tier with up to 100,000 subscribers and 300 emails daily. Paid plans start at $25/month. Drip has no free tier and starts at $39/month, making it more expensive for beginners.

faq.q: Can I switch from Sendinblue to Drip?
faq.a: Yes, you can migrate between platforms. Both tools allow you to export your contact lists and email templates. Plan the migration carefully, especially if you have complex automation workflows, as Drip's e-commerce-specific features may require reconfiguration.
